name: My back hurts from lifting at work but I had back problems before, will workers comp cover it in Boise?

mainEntity.name: My back hurts from lifting at work but I had back problems before, will workers comp cover it in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, coverage possible for work-aggravated pre-existing back issues in UT/ID with medical proof of worsening.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, Utah and Idaho workers' comp can cover aggravation of pre-existing conditions if work caused the worsening.[2] Insurers often deny back injuries claiming prior issues, but proof of job aggravation qualifies you for benefits.[2] Key factors: (1) Medical evidence linking work to aggravation; (2) Disclose all prior records honestly; (3) Insurers request 10 years of history pre-litigation; (4) Cumulative trauma needs strong work nexus proof.[1][2] Get a doctor's note tying it to work, report promptly, and don't sign broad medical releases without advice. Seek experienced guidance to fight denials.

description: Yes, coverage possible for work-aggravated pre-existing back issues in UT/ID with medical proof of worsening.[2]
